name: Dollar Bill Symbols
description: The concept of 'Dollar Bill Symbols' refers to the interpretation of various symbols found on the United States one-dollar bill, often associated with Freemasonry, the Illuminati, and other secret societies. Proponents believe these symbols, such as the All-Seeing Eye on the pyramid, the eagle holding a banner with the Latin phrase 'E Pluribus Unum,' and the Roman numerals MDCCLXXVI (1776), are evidence of a hidden influence on American history and government. Some theories suggest these symbols point to a New World Order or a secret agenda for global domination. The pyramid, in particular, is frequently linked to ancient Egyptian mysteries and esoteric knowledge. The reverse of the Great Seal, featuring the unfinished pyramid and the eye, was approved by Congress in 1782. Mainstream historical and numismatic perspectives generally attribute these symbols to the influence of classical and Enlightenment-era symbolism, the founders' interest in republican ideals, and the need for national symbols representing unity and strength. The All-Seeing Eye, for instance, is seen as a representation of divine providence, not necessarily a Masonic symbol, though it was later adopted by Freemasonry. The unfinished pyramid signifies the ongoing growth and potential of the nation. Critics of conspiracy theories argue that these interpretations are often based on selective readings of history, misinterpretations of symbolism, and a lack of understanding of the historical context in which these symbols were adopted. Many of the symbols were common during the time of the founding fathers and represented the ideals of the Enlightenment.
subtitle: Hidden meanings in plain sight?

claims.claim: The All-Seeing Eye represents the Illuminati's control.
claims.evidence: The presence of the All-Seeing Eye atop an unfinished pyramid on the Great Seal.
claims.criticism: The All-Seeing Eye was a common symbol of divine providence and enlightenment ideals, predating the Illuminati's popularization.

claims.claim: The pyramid symbolizes ancient Egyptian mysteries and occult knowledge.
claims.evidence: The pyramid's association with ancient Egypt and esoteric traditions.
claims.criticism: The pyramid was chosen as a symbol of strength, endurance, and the nation's ongoing growth, inspired by classical architecture and ideals.

claims.claim: The number 13 and its multiples are intentionally repeated to signify a hidden agenda.
claims.evidence: 13 stars above the eagle, 13 steps on the pyramid, 13 stripes on the shield.
claims.criticism: The number 13 represents the original 13 colonies and is a natural representation of the founding states.

keyFigures.name: William Josiah Sutton
keyFigures.role: Popularized theories about Masonic influence on the dollar bill.

keyFigures.name: David Ovason
keyFigures.role: Author of "The Secret Architecture of Our Nation's Capital", analyzing symbols.

keyFigures.name: Joseph Uscinski
keyFigures.role: Political scientist specializing in conspiracy theories who debunks many claims.

relatedConcepts: Great Seal of the United States

timeline.event: Roman numerals MDCCLXXVI appear on the Great Seal, representing the year of American Independence.
Congress approves the design of the Great Seal, including the unfinished pyramid and All-Seeing Eye.
The Great Seal is added to the reverse of the one-dollar bill.
Theories about hidden meanings in the dollar bill symbols become popular online and in alternative media.

sources.author: David Ovason
sources.work: The Secret Architecture of Our Nation's Capital
sources.title: David Ovason

sources.author: Richard S. Patterson and Richardson Dougall
sources.work: The Eagle and the Shield: A History of the Great Seal of the United States
sources.title: Richard S. Patterson and Richardson Dougall
